title: crataegus oxyacantha var. kyrtostyla
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha var. kyrtostyla, also known as the Midland hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn plant, a deciduous shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The leaves and flowers of the Midland hawthorn possess medicinal properties, particularly its flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have been used to treat cardiovascular conditions. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and preventing cardiac arrhythmias. Midland hawthorn can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, allowing for its active compounds to be absorbed and utilized by the body.
